purpose: Workstream C fixtures are a two-sided proof: the fixture must be demonstrably RED against pre-fix main, then flipped to enforced-green by the fix bead. This file records the red half so the flip is auditable rather than asserted.

expected_failure.flipped_green_by: A2a (map FileStoreError::Locked to a distinct code before the catch-all)
expected_failure.flip_action: delete the #[ignore]; the assertions must not change. A2a may pick any name in ACTIONABLE_LOCK_CODES or extend that list, but may not keep emitting the generic write failure

red_run.result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 1 filtered out
red_run.panic_message: `setup --write` must report that the state store is locked, using one of ["ORACLEMCP_STATE_STORE_LOCKED", "ORACLEMCP_STATE_LOCKED", "ORACLEMCP_SERVICE_RUNNING"]
red_run.root_cause: crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs folds ConfigOpsError::FileStore(_) and ConfigOpsError::Io(_) into a catch-all arm emitting ORACLEMCP_SETUP_WRITE_FAILED with the fixed text 'config workflow failed before completion'. FileStoreError::Locked -- the one variant that identifies a live owner and implies a specific remedy -- is discarded at that last step.
red_run.consequence: The operator sees the same code and the same sentence they would get for a full disk, an unwritable path, or a failed validation. The natural next moves are retry or hand-editing the config; the running server is never suspected. This is the first thing an operator hits when reconfiguring a live service.

green_half.result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
green_half.note: The floor: both verbs must FAIL under contention rather than silently appearing to succeed. That already holds, and pinning it stops a diagnostic fix from accidentally turning the collision into a success path.

why_no_existing_test_caught_it.reason: Every file_store test runs offline with a single actor and no contention, and the operator-API tests call handlers in-process where the lock is already held by the caller. Contention between a live server and a separate CLI process is the configuration no test creates.
why_no_existing_test_caught_it.fixture_technique: The lock is taken by the test process via FileStore::acquire_service_owner rather than by spawning serve. flock is held per open file description, so a child process opening the same lock file contends identically -- this keeps the fixture offline, fast, and free of a server readiness race while asserting the same CLI behaviour under contention.
